tool_input.summary: No dedicated PDS dataset matching a 'Saturn plasmoid/reconnection event catalog' (i.e., a published list of reconnection/plasmoid times with inferred mass loss) was found in the local PDS catalog search. To evaluate whether reconnection-related mass loss can balance Enceladus’ plasma source, you will likely need to (1) detect events from Cassini MAG/CAPS/RPWS/MIMI time series, (2) estimate plasmoid content and occurrence rate, and (3) compare to Enceladus source estimates from INMS/plasma data. Below are the most relevant PDS4 and PDS3 archives that support that analysis.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary dataset to identify reconnection signatures (plasmoids, dipolarizations, current sheet crossings) and to compute B-field needed for pressure/flux estimates; includes 1 s and 1 min products plus full-resolution RTN/KRTP at Saturn.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw MAG if you need highest time resolution and to apply custom calibration/processing for event detection or uncertainty analysis.

candidates.title: CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1SECAVG V2.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3-era 1-second averaged Cassini MAG summary data; useful if reproducing legacy Saturn reconnection/plasmoid studies that used PDS3 volumes.

candidates.title: CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1MINAVG V2.1
candidates.reasoning: PDS3-era 1-minute averaged Cassini MAG summary data; convenient for long-duration event catalogs and occurrence-rate statistics.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived plasma quantities (moments, spacecraft potential products/collections) that help estimate density/flow during tail events and plasmoid content.

candidates.title: Cassini Orbiter Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Raw Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Underlying CAPS measurements needed if you want to recompute moments/compose event-integrated mass loss across the plasma sheet.

candidates.title: Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Precomputed forward-model fit parameters from CAPS (1999–2012) that can speed event-by-event density/temperature/flow estimates for mass-loss calculations.

candidates.title: CO E J S SW CAPS 2 UNCALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 CAPS uncalibrated archive referenced by later derived products; useful for reproducing older event analyses and cross-checking fits.

candidates.title: CO E J S SW CAPS 3 CALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 CAPS calibrated archive for Saturn plasma properties relevant to plasmoid content/mass.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Independent electron density estimates to constrain plasma content in/around plasmoids and to compare inner-magnetosphere density variations tied to Enceladus mass loading.

candidates.title: CO SS S RPWS 5 NEPROXY V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 RPWS electron density proxy product; supports long time-series density context for reconnection intervals and global mass budget studies.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic ion/electron context (CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS) often used to identify injections and tail dynamics associated with reconnection and plasmoids.

candidates.title: CO S MIMI 4 INCA CALIB V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 calibrated INCA energetic neutral atom/ion products that can provide global context for mass transport/loss during reconnection intervals.

candidates.title: CO S INMS 3 L1A U V2.0
candidates.reasoning: INMS Level 1A archive needed to quantify Enceladus-related neutral/ion composition and constrain source rates for comparison against tail mass loss.

candidates.title: CASSINI SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels for spacecraft/planet geometry; required to place detected plasmoids in Saturn’s magnetotail coordinates and compute volumes/flux tube content during events.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Is the mass loss inferred from the detected Saturn plasmoid/reconnection event catalog sufficient to balance Enceladus as a plasma source?

dataset.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes 1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute averaged data is provided as well.

dataset.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
dataset.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies. When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density. At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.
instruments: RADIO AND PLASMA WAVE SCIENCE for CO

dataset.description: The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime mission. The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47 UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers data received during extended missions. Sampling rates were variable and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities on-board.
